title: taudit Pipeline Baseline
description: Per-pipeline baseline file written by `taudit baseline init` and updated by `taudit baseline accept`. Stored at `.taudit/baselines/<pipeline-content-hash>.json`. Diffed by `taudit baseline diff` and `taudit verify`. Stable contract — additive 1.x.y changes are non-breaking; 2.0.0 means breaking changes.

schema_version.description: Semver of this schema. Must start with `1.` for now.

pipeline_path.description: Path of the pipeline as the user sees it (e.g. `.github/workflows/release.yml`). Informational; the canonical key is `pipeline_content_hash`.

pipeline_content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the pipeline file's bytes at `init` time, prefixed with `sha256:`. Renaming the pipeline file preserves the baseline because the hash key is stable.

pipeline_identity_material_hash.description: Optional additive hash of parser-emitted dependency-like material (include/template/repository declarations and delegation signals). Missing on legacy baselines; when present and mismatched at verify time, suppression is skipped and baseline should be recaptured.

captured_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which this baseline was first written.

captured_by.description: Identity that ran `taudit baseline init` (typically a git author email).

taudit_version.description: Semver of the taudit binary that produced this baseline.

rules_version.description: Free-form description of the rule set at capture time (e.g. `32-builtin`, `32-builtin+5-custom`).

baseline_findings.description: Sorted ASC by `fingerprint` for deterministic git diffs.

fingerprint.description: 32-hex SHA-256-truncated-to-128-bit fingerprint (v3 algorithm) matching the SARIF `partialFingerprints.primaryLocationLineHash`, the JSON `findings[].fingerprint`, and the CloudEvents `tauditfindingfingerprint` extension. The shared test `baseline_fingerprint_matches_sarif_fingerprint` enforces byte-equality across formats. Widened from 16 hex (64-bit) in v1.1.0-beta.3 to close a birthday-collision attack against `.taudit-suppressions.yml`.

rule_id.description: Snake-case rule id. Either a custom rule id parsed from a `[id] message` prefix, or the snake_case `FindingCategory`.

severity.description: Severity captured at `init` time. Drives the critical-bypass check.

first_seen_at.description: When this entry was first added (init or accept).

reason_waived.description: Free-form justification. Required (>=10 chars) when entry is added by `taudit baseline accept`. `null` for plain pre-existing entries from `init`.

severity_override.description: Acknowledges that a Critical finding is being bypassed intentionally. Required for any waiver of an originally-critical finding; if absent, the critical falls through to exit 1 even if it appears in the baseline.

expires_at.description: Hard deadline for the waiver. Mandatory when `severity_override = critical`; must be <=90 days from `first_seen_at`. Past this timestamp the waiver no longer protects.
